question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 63.00% on 2026-03-27 for the Metaculus question "Will the United States conduct a ground invasion of Iran before 2027?"?
summary: The CP for the US ground invasion of Iran question is currently exactly at the 63% threshold (as of March 14). The US-Iran war that began Feb 28 has been actively escalating with no diplomatic off-ramps: Iran's new Supreme Leader rejected all ceasefire proposals, the US is deploying Marines to the region, and specific ground operation targets (Kharg Island, Strait of Hormuz) are being actively discussed. These developments since the March 14 CP snapshot provide upward catalysts. Manifold markets show rising ground invasion probability (76%). However, the CP already absorbed the massive war-starting shock (1.7% to 64%), then slightly declined, suggesting some forecasters see a full ground invasion as unlikely despite the air campaign. Strong public opposition and expert consensus against invasion could anchor the CP. With the threshold exactly at the current CP, even marginal upward movement resolves YES, which tips the balance. I estimate 60% probability that CP will be above 63% on March 27.

factors.description: Active US-Iran war ongoing since Feb 28, with no ceasefire in sight - both sides rejecting negotiations as of March 17. War is escalating, not de-escalating, which pushes forecasters toward higher CP.

US Marine Expeditionary Unit (2,500 troops) actively deploying to the region; 82nd Airborne on standby; Trump not ruling out ground troops. These concrete military preparations are new developments since CP was last measured.

Israeli military spokesperson discussed comprehensive attack plans coordinated with US for period before April 1 (Passover). Specific ground operation targets identified (Kharg Island, Strait of Hormuz, uranium stockpiles).
Manifold market on US ground deployment to Iran jumped from 61.3% to 76.4% between March 15-16, showing prediction markets are pricing in rising probability of ground operations.
CP slightly declined from 64% to 63% between March 7-14, suggesting some forecasters may be skeptical despite ongoing escalation. The CP has already priced in the war starting.

Strong analytical consensus and public opposition (60-74%) against ground invasion. Military experts note 500k-1M troops needed for full invasion, making it impractical. This could anchor skeptical forecasters.
Threshold is exactly at current CP (63%). Even tiny upward movement crosses it - the bar for YES is very low. Any net escalation in the news cycle is enough.

reasoning:
My forecast: **0.60 (60%)** probability that the CP will be higher than 63% on March 27.

**Key reasoning:**
- The US-Iran war is actively escalating with no ceasefire in sight
- Concrete military preparations for ground operations (Marines deploying, 82nd Airborne on standby) are new developments since the March 14 CP snapshot
- The threshold is exactly at the current CP — even marginal upward movement resolves YES
- However, the CP already absorbed the massive war shock (1.7% → 64%) and slightly declined to 63%, suggesting some forecasters are skeptical of ground invasion despite the air campaign
- Strong public opposition (60-74%) and expert consensus that full invasion is impractical could anchor the CP

The net assessment tilts toward YES because the escalation trajectory is clear and the bar is very low (just needs to exceed 63.00%), but it's not a high-confidence call given the CP has been drifting slightly downward even as the war continues.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/38768) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-03-27 14:37:15 is higher than 63.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 63.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-03-27 14:37:15, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
